page: Microsoft Azure D128lds_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Dldsv7 family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with 128 vCPUs mapped to 64 physical cores using Microsoft Hyper-V virtualization. Operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz, the x86_64 architecture features 480 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for hardware-accelerated matrix operations.

The instance is configured with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. This resource density represents a clear tradeoff, prioritizing raw computational throughput over memory-intensive workloads. However, the server includes a substantial 7559 GB of local SSD storage and is premium storage capable, providing high-capacity local disk access for data-heavy operations.

Benchmark data highlights top-tier performance across multiple domains. The multi-core stress-ng CPU performance and PassMark CPU score rank in the top 10%, supported by high memory read bandwidth of up to 10709.34 GB/sec for cached blocks. Database operations and Redis SET performance are also in the top tier, with Redis achieving 196.23 million operations per second. For machine learning workloads, the hardware-assisted AMX instructions enable strong prompt processing speeds, reaching 40.29 tokens per second on a 70B parameter model, though text generation speeds remain average.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D128lds_v7 offers high compute density and large local storage capacity relative to its memory footprint, making it a cost-effective choice for compute-bound tasks that do not require massive RAM arrays. It is highly suited for high-throughput web serving, multi-threaded decompression, compute-heavy database operations, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D128lds_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the Dldsv7 family, featuring 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz. Built on x86_64 architecture, it includes 256.0 GB of RAM, representing a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. The instance is bundled with 7559 GB of local storage and supports premium storage. Benchmark results place this server in the top tier for multi-core CPU performance, database operations, and Redis SET throughput, which reaches 196.23 million operations per second. It also demonstrates strong performance in CPU-based LLM prompt processing due to Intel AMX support. The D128lds_v7 is highly suited for compute-intensive workloads, high-throughput web serving, and local-storage-heavy database applications that do not require high memory-to-CPU ratios.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D128lds_v7 is a compute-optimized server with 128 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 7559 GB local storage. It features an Intel Xeon processor and delivers top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D128lds_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine with 128 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 7559 GB local storage for high-throughput workloads.
tagline: A high-vCPU compute-optimized server featuring massive local storage and advanced instruction sets for demanding multi-threaded processing and database workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 128-vCPU Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz.
Equipped with 256 GB of RAM and 7559 GB of high-capacity local storage.
Delivers top-tier multi-core CPU, database, and Redis benchmark performance.
Supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 for accelerated matrix and vector operations.
Best suited for compute-heavy databases, web serving, and CPU-based inference.
